document.title: Type Certificate Certified to LAIRD CONNECTIVITY INC
authorityOrJurisdiction: Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications
standards: Article 38-24, Paragraph 1 of the Radio Law

sourceSummary.oneLine: Type certificate for LAIRD CONNECTIVITY INC specified radio equipment models ST60-2230C and related variants under Radio Law provisions.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used as evidence that construction type certification was granted for the listed 2.4GHz wide band low power data communication system models and to support conformity mark and inspection record obligations.
keyPoints: Certified Number is 003-170090 and Certified Date is March 3, 2021.
Classification is Article 2-1-19, 2.4GHz Wide Band low power data communication system.
The certificate states certification by type was granted in accordance with Article 38-24, Paragraph 1 of the Radio Law.
Certified dealers must inspect products before shipping, make inspection records, and retain the records for 10 years.
The conformity mark may be affixed visibly or displayed electronically under stated certification regulation methods.

entities.name: LAIRD CONNECTIVITY INC
entities.evidence: Certified to LAIRD CONNECTIVITY INC; Vendor Name LAIRD CONNECTIVITY INC

entities.name: DSP Research, Inc.
entities.evidence: Entities who have received a construction type certification from DSP Research are defined as a “certified dealer” under the Radio Law.

entities.name: Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications
entities.evidence: The Minister of Internal Affairs and Communications can prohibit the certified dealer from affixing the conformity mark on the radio equipment

entities.name: Radio Law
entities.evidence: certification by type has been granted in accordance with the provisions of Article 38-24, Paragraph 1 of the Radio Law

concepts.evidence: Article 2-1-19 2.4GHz Wide Band low power data communication system

concepts.evidence: Certification label, technical conformity mark (GITEKI mark), etc.

concepts.name: Certified dealer
concepts.evidence: Entities who have received a construction type certification from DSP Research are defined as a “certified dealer” under the Radio Law.

concepts.name: Inspection record retention
concepts.evidence: Certified dealer must then “make record of the inspection results” and “retain the inspection records for 10 years.”

evidence.claim: The certificate was issued to LAIRD CONNECTIVITY INC.
The certified equipment is classified as Article 2-1-19, 2.4GHz Wide Band low power data communication system.
evidence.evidenceText: Article 2-1-19 2.4GHz Wide Band low power data communication system
evidence.pageTarget: 2.4GHz Wide Band Low Power Data Communication System

evidence.claim: Certified Number 003-170090 applies to the certificate.
The certificate was granted in accordance with Article 38-24, Paragraph 1 of the Radio Law.
evidence.evidenceText: This is to certify that the above mentioned certification by type has been granted in accordance with the provisions of Article 38-24, Paragraph 1 of the Radio Law.

evidence.claim: Certified dealers must inspect certified products before shipping and retain inspection records for 10 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Before shipping the certified product, a certified dealer shall “inspect” to make sure the product has performance identical to that of the certified construction type. Certified dealer must then “make record of the inspection results” and “retain the inspection records for 10 years.”

evidence.claim: The conformity mark may be electronically displayed with documentation describing the display method.
evidence.evidenceText: When electronically affixing the mark on either the specified radio equipment or the product that has built-in radio equipment with conformity mark... it is necessary to attach documentation stating the conformity mark is visually electronically displayed with a description detailing the display method

reviewFlags.reason: Source text appears to include OCR or extraction artifacts, including repeated DSPRR2 headers and fragmented 5GHz-band wording.
Authority signal metadata is general, while the source text references Radio Law, Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications, and DSP Research, Inc.
No explicit issuing signature or named official is present in the extracted source text.
